summary: US President Donald Trump stated that Chinese President Xi Jinping offered help reopening the Strait of Hormuz and pledged not to supply military equipment to Iran during their Beijing summit. Trump also extended a White House invitation to Xi for September.
contents:
## Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Outcomes

### Hormuz and Iran Pledges

President Trump disclosed on May 14, 2026, that President Xi Jinping made two significant commitments during their Beijing summit. Xi reportedly offered Chinese assistance in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, telling Trump that if he could help in any way, he would like to do so. Xi also strongly pledged not to provide military equipment to Iran, a key US demand given concerns about Chinese arms flowing to Tehran during the ongoing conflict.

### Strategic Implications

The commitments, if implemented, would represent a major shift in Chinese posture toward the Iran conflict. China had previously maintained a neutral stance while continuing to purchase Iranian oil. Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed that the two leaders discussed the Strait of Hormuz, Taiwan, and broader military tensions during the state visit.

### September Invitation

Trump extended a White House invitation to Xi for a September visit, signaling continued high-level engagement between the two powers. The summit occurred as three US aircraft carrier strike groups maintained positions in the Middle East enforcing the Iran blockade, and as the IMF warned the global economy moved toward an adverse scenario due to sustained Iran war disruptions to energy markets and trade flows.

title: Armed Forces of Philippines Reports 35 Chinese Vessels Across Four Key West Philippine Sea Features
summary: The AFP monitored 35 Chinese vessels including 15 PLA Navy warships and 20 Coast Guard boats operating near Scarborough Shoal, Ayungin Shoal, Escoda Shoal, and Pag-asa Island from May 4 to 11 during the Balikatan 2026 joint military exercises.
contents:
## Chinese Maritime Presence in West Philippine Sea

### Vessel Distribution

The Armed Forces of the Philippines reported on May 13, 2026, that military surveillance tracked 35 Chinese vessels across four key features in the West Philippine Sea over the preceding week. The largest concentration appeared at Panatag (Scarborough) Shoal, where monitoring detected eight People's Liberation Army Navy warships and nine China Coast Guard vessels.

### Feature-by-Feature Breakdown

At Ayungin (Second Thomas) Shoal, observers counted one PLAN warship and six CCG vessels. Near Escoda (Sabina) Shoal, three PLAN ships and two CCG vessels maintained positions. Around Pag-asa (Thitu) Island, three PLAN warships and three CCG vessels operated in the vicinity. The total of 35 vessels marked a decrease from 62 Chinese vessels monitored across the same features during April.

### Balikatan Context

AFP spokesperson Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad noted that no coercive or aggressive actions occurred from the Chinese vessels during the Balikatan 2026 exercises, which concluded May 8. However, Trinidad reported that China issued narratives claiming it conducted combat exercises near Scarborough Shoal, though the AFP detected no synchronized military activity supporting those claims. Japan, Australia, and Canada participated alongside US and Philippine forces in this year's drills.

title: CENTCOM Reports 70 Commercial Vessels Redirected and Four Disabled in Arabian Sea Blockade Enforcement
summary: US Central Command announced that forces enforcing the Iran blockade have redirected 70 commercial vessels and disabled four others in ongoing maritime enforcement operations in the Arabian Sea. USS Truxtun supported operations with helicopter assets.
contents:
## US Maritime Blockade Enforcement Update

### Operational Scope

US Central Command disclosed on May 14, 2026, that its forces have redirected 70 commercial vessels and disabled four others as part of ongoing maritime enforcement operations targeting traffic linked to Iranian ports. The announcement represented a significant escalation from the 34 vessels redirected when the blockade initially took effect in mid-April.

### Naval Assets

The guided-missile destroyer USS Truxtun (DDG-103) conducted operations in the Arabian Sea while an MH-60 Sea Hawk helicopter from Helicopter Maritime Strike Squadron 50 supported enforcement activities from its flight deck. The blockade encompasses the entirety of the Iranian coast and operates primarily in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz.

### Strategic Context

The enforcement operation, designated Project Freedom, involves guided-missile destroyers, over 100 land and sea-based aircraft, multi-domain unmanned platforms, and approximately 15,000 service members. Two carrier strike groups led by USS Abraham Lincoln and USS George H.W. Bush remain on station to support blockade operations. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the blockade would continue for as long as President Trump directed, emphasizing that every vessel meeting US criteria had either complied or faced redirection.

title: US Energy Secretary Warns Iran Weeks Away From Weapons-Grade Uranium Enrichment Capability
summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee that Iran stands a small number of weeks from enriching uranium to weapons-grade levels. Wright called Iran's stockpile of uranium enriched to 60 percent very concerning and endorsed eliminating the entire program.
contents:
## Congressional Testimony on Iran Nuclear Status

### Wright's Assessment

US Energy Secretary Chris Wright delivered a stark warning to the Senate Armed Services Committee on May 14, 2026, stating that Iran remains weeks away from enriching its uranium stockpile to weapons-grade levels. Wright specified that enrichment from the current 60 percent to the 90 percent weapons-grade threshold could happen in a small number of weeks, though a separate weaponization process would follow.

### Stockpile Concerns

Senator Richard Blumenthal pressed Wright on the scope of Iran's enriched uranium holdings. Wright confirmed Iran possesses uranium enriched to 60 percent and a large quantity at 20 percent, calling both levels very concerning. The International Atomic Energy Agency estimated Iran's enriched uranium stockpile at 9,874.9 kilograms as of June 2025, but the agency currently lacks access to verify current stockpile status.

### Policy Implications

Wright endorsed pursuing the elimination of Iran's entire nuclear program, including both preventing future enrichment and addressing existing stockpiles. The testimony gained additional urgency after Iranian parliamentary spokesman Ebrahim Rezaei wrote on social media that 90 percent enrichment represented one of Iran's options if attacked again. The IAEA has maintained zero access to Iran's declared enrichment facilities since Tehran terminated all agency access on February 28, 2026.

title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 22 People Including Eight Children Across Southern Lebanon
summary: Israeli air and drone strikes hit approximately 40 locations across southern Lebanon on May 13, killing 22 people including eight children. Three strikes targeted vehicles on the coastal highway near Beirut while further attacks struck multiple southern villages.
contents:
## Israeli Strikes Across Southern Lebanon

### Coastal Highway Attacks

Israeli drones targeted three vehicles along the coastal highway between 20 and 30 kilometers south of Beirut on May 13, 2026, killing eight people including two children. The strikes hit cars near the towns of Barja, Jiyeh, and Saadiyat. Four additional fatalities resulted from strikes on vehicles further south in Sidon, Maaliyeh, Chaaitiyeh, and Naqoura.

### Village Strikes

Later on the same day, Israeli strikes killed six people in the village of Arab Salim, three in Roumine, and one in Harouf, all in southern Lebanon. The victims included six children according to Lebanese health ministry updates.

### Scale of Destruction

The Israeli military stated it struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities and rocket launchers across multiple areas. The Lebanese state news agency reported Israeli airstrikes pounded around 40 locations in the country's south and east. Chadi Abdallah, head of Lebanon's National Council for Scientific Research, reported that over 10,000 homes have suffered damage or destruction since the ceasefire took effect, with 5,386 housing units completely razed and 5,246 damaged.

### UNIFIL Concerns

The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon expressed increasing concern about activities near UN positions, citing the increased use of drones that produced explosions in and around peacekeeping bases, putting personnel at risk. Six UNIFIL peacekeepers have died since the conflict began on March 2.

title: Israel Awards Elbit Systems $34M Contract to Extend F-35I Adir Range Amid Iran War
summary: Israel's defense ministry announced plans to develop external fuel tanks for its F-35I Adir fleet, awarding Elbit Systems a $34 million contract to adapt Cyclone-design tanks from the F-16I Sufa platform.
contents:
## F-35I Range Extension Program

Israel's defense ministry announced on May 14 a development program to extend the combat range of its F-35I Adir stealth fighters. Elbit Systems received a contract valued at approximately $34 million (100 million NIS) to develop and implement external fuel tanks adapted from the existing Cyclone design used on the F-16I Sufa variant.

## Strategic Context

The announcement arrived amid a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, roughly one month after Operation Lion's Roar struck targets deep inside Iranian territory. During that operation, an F-35I scored the first air-to-air kill ever credited to an F-35 in combat, downing an Iranian Yak-130 light-attack aircraft.

## Fleet Expansion

Israel currently operates 50 F-35I aircraft across two squadrons and has begun receiving 25 additional jets ordered in 2023. Earlier this month, Israel opened negotiations to purchase 25 more aircraft, which would bring the total fleet to 100 and expand the force to four squadrons.

## Operational Implications

Extending the F-35I's range addresses a critical capability gap exposed during recent long-range strike operations against Iranian targets. The current F-35A variant offers approximately 1,200 miles of range, and the adapted external fuel tanks aim to push that threshold significantly further, enabling sustained deep-strike missions without extensive aerial refueling support.

link_preview.title: Israel to extend F-35I range amid war with Iran
link_preview.description: Elbit Systems was awarded the development contract, valued at approximately $34 million (100 million NIS).

title: CEPA Report Warns Europe Faces Sustained Russian Shadow Warfare Campaign With Escalation Bias
summary: The Center for European Policy Analysis published a major study documenting Russia's sustained shadow warfare campaign against European infrastructure, identifying systemic deterrence failures across NATO allies.
contents:
## Shadow Warfare Assessment

The Center for European Policy Analysis (CEPA) released on March 31 a comprehensive study titled "War Without End: Deterring Russia's Shadow War," documenting how Russian-linked actors have sabotaged critical infrastructure, disrupted aviation and energy systems, penetrated digital networks, and targeted political opponents across Europe. CEPA researchers presented recommendations to EEAS representatives and NATO Parliamentary Assembly members in Brussels during mid-April.

## Systemic Pattern of Aggression

The report identified a coordinated campaign spanning multiple domains: infiltration through criminal proxies, sabotage of undersea cables and energy connections, and persistent cyber operations targeting operational systems rather than merely information technology. CEPA characterized this activity not as temporary hybrid adaptation but as "a system of conflict rooted in ideology, embedded in institutions, and biased toward escalation."

## Four Deterrence Bottlenecks

CEPA's research identified four critical failures: shadow warfare systematically gets misclassified through civilian enforcement frameworks; response tempo lags behind attack tempo; fear of escalation produces ambiguity favoring the aggressor; and deterrence remains tethered to courtroom standards of proof that shadow warfare specifically frustrates.

## NB8 Ministerial Response

Nordic-Baltic foreign ministers meeting in Kuressaare, Estonia on April 29-30 pledged to strengthen resilience against Russian hybrid activities, deepen cooperation through the Joint Expeditionary Force, and increase costs for those responsible for shadow warfare operations, including proxies.

link_preview.title: War Without End: Deterring Russia's Shadow War
link_preview.description: Either Europe will continue allowing Russia's shadow war to set the terms of escalation, or it will act now to prevent a larger war.

title: South Korea Announces Annual Taegeuk Military Exercises Next Week to Counter North Korean Threats
summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff confirmed the five-day Taegeuk computer-simulated command post exercise will begin May 19, focusing on crisis management against evolving North Korean threats.
contents:
## Exercise Announcement

South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff announced on May 15 that the military will stage its annual computer-simulated Taegeuk exercise beginning Monday, May 19. The five-day command post exercise will focus on applying training scenarios that reflect changes in the security environment and the evolving nature of recent warfare.

## Enhanced Threat Response

The military aims to strengthen its crisis management and response capabilities against a wide range of North Korean threats. The announcement follows North Korea's April firing of multiple short-range ballistic missiles toward the East Sea, which Pyongyang later described as a test-firing of cluster bombs.

## Broader Security Context

The exercises come amid heightened tensions following North Korea's constitutional amendments removing reunification language and its accelerated military buildup. Kim Jong Un recently visited munitions factories, announcing plans to deploy a new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzer along the southern border with a striking range exceeding 60 kilometers — enough to place Seoul within firing range.

## ROK-US Alliance Dynamics

Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back acknowledged perception differences with Washington regarding operational control (OPCON) transfer during a May 12 press briefing, while National Security Adviser Wi Sung-lac stated South Korea aims to complete an OPCON recovery roadmap this year.

link_preview.title: S. Korea to stage regular Taegeuk military drills next week to counter N.K. threats | Yonhap News Agency
link_preview.description: SEOUL, May 15 (Yonhap) -- The military will stage its annual computer-simulated Taegeuk ex...

title: Hezbollah Drone Attack Wounds Three Israeli Civilians Near Rosh HaNikra Border Area
summary: A Hezbollah drone struck a parking lot near Israel's border with Lebanon, injuring three civilians including two seriously, in what the IDF described as a blatant ceasefire violation.
contents:
## Attack Details

A Hezbollah unmanned aerial vehicle struck a parking lot close to the Israel-Lebanon border near Rosh HaNikra on May 14, injuring three Israeli civilians. The Galilee Medical Center in Nahariya treated two people in serious condition and one with light injuries. No air raid sirens sounded in the area, indicating the drone evaded military detection systems.

## IDF Response

The Israel Defense Forces characterized the drone attack as "a blatant violation of the ceasefire understandings by the Hezbollah terror organization." The IDF launched a wave of airstrikes on Hezbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon after issuing evacuation warnings for eight villages: Libbaya, Sohmor, Tefahta, Kfar Melki, Yohmor al-Beqaa, Ain al-Tineh, Houmine al-Faouqa, and Mazraat Sinay.

## Ongoing Clashes

The drone represented one of several projectiles Hezbollah launched at Israel and at IDF troops in southern Lebanon on May 14, including anti-tank guided missiles and mortars. Several additional drones launched by the group met interception by IDF air defense systems.

## Diplomatic Track

The attack occurred hours before a third round of Israeli-Lebanese talks in Washington, where delegations planned to discuss Hezbollah disarmament pathways. Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter indicated Israel would present Beirut with a framework to gradually disarm Hezbollah while pursuing broader peace negotiations.

link_preview.title: 4 civilians hurt in Hezbollah attack on north as Israel, Lebanon set for 3rd round of talks
link_preview.description: One listed in critical condition; IDF strikes several towns after issuing warning; Israel to present framework for disarmament, long-term ties at highest level talks in decades

title: China Highlights Service Sector Opening at Economic Roundtable as Trump-Xi Summit Continues in Beijing
summary: Xinhua News Agency reported on China's Economic Roundtable featuring senior officials from the NDRC and State Council discussing deeper service sector opening, coinciding with President Trump's high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.
contents:
## Economic Roundtable Discussion

Xinhua News Agency highlighted a #ChinaEconomicRoundtable event on May 15, featuring senior Chinese officials discussing plans to expand service sector opening. Li Chunfang of the National Development and Reform Commission stated that developing the service sector aligns with industry demands and public aspirations. Liu Tao of the Development Research Center of the State Council explained that China's policy efforts focus on easing foreign investment access, expanding opening-up platforms, and deepening opening in key sectors.

## Industry Perspective

Zhang Chengcheng, chairman of Roffar Elderly Service Co., Ltd., argued that deeper opening in the service sector can help build influential "China Service" brands, suggesting the private sector sees significant opportunity in expanded market access for foreign competitors and partners.

## Trump-Xi Summit Context

The roundtable discussion coincided with President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The summit agenda covered trade tensions, the ongoing Iran conflict, and regional security matters. The economic roundtable's focus on service sector liberalization appeared designed to signal China's willingness to address longstanding trade grievances around market access.

## Broader Diplomatic Dynamics

Ukraine's Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha urged both Trump and Xi to exert pressure on Russia to end the war in Ukraine, noting the summit's timing amid massive Russian strikes on Ukrainian cities. The convergence of economic diplomacy and security crises underscored the interconnected nature of the global challenges facing both powers.

link_preview.title: China Xinhua News on X
link_preview.description: Zhang Chengcheng, chairman of Roffar Elderly Service Co., Ltd., says deeper opening up in the service sector can help build influential "China Service" brands. #ChinaEconomicRoundtable https://t.co/DiAxxS5fSn

title: Ukraine Reports 168 Combat Engagements on May 14 as Russia Launches Massive Overnight Strikes on Kyiv
summary: The Ukrainian General Staff recorded 168 combat engagements on May 14, with almost half occurring in the Pokrovsk sector, while Russia launched over 670 drones and 56 missiles targeting Kyiv and other regions overnight.
contents:
## Frontline Activity

The General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine reported 168 combat engagements on May 14 between Ukrainian defenders and Russian troops. The Pokrovsk sector saw the heaviest fighting with 74 enemy assault and offensive actions repelled near multiple settlements including Stara Mykolaivka, Yelyzavetivka, and Myroliubivka. The Novopavlivka sector recorded 26 attacks, while the Toretsk sector experienced 19 engagements.

## Aerial Bombardment

Russian forces launched one missile strike and 68 air strikes using 108 guided bombs against Ukrainian positions and settlements. The invaders also employed 3,327 kamikaze drones and carried out 5,298 shelling attacks, including 151 using multiple rocket launcher systems. President Zelensky reported more than 670 drones and 56 missiles targeted the country overnight, among the largest attacks since the 2022 full-scale invasion.

## Kyiv Destruction

At least 16 people died, including two children, in Kyiv after a Kh-101 cruise missile struck an apartment building in the Darnytskyi district, causing a partial collapse. Rescuers continued searching for at least 20 people feared missing under the rubble. The city declared Friday a day of mourning.

## Post-Ceasefire Escalation

The massive strikes marked the third consecutive day of deadly attacks following the expiration of a US-brokered three-day ceasefire on May 11. The Ukrainian Air Force reported a 93% interception rate for drones but only 73% for missiles, with ballistic missile defense remaining the primary challenge.

link_preview.title: War update: 168 combat engagements on frontline over past day
link_preview.description: On May 14, a total of 168 combat engagements took place between Ukrainian defenders and Russian troops, with almost half of them occurring in the Pokrovsk sector. — Ukrinform.

title: Cisco Talos Tracks Active Exploitation of SD-WAN Vulnerabilities by Sophisticated APT Actor UAT-8616
summary: Cisco Talos disclosed ongoing exploitation of CVE-2026-20182 in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN by a highly sophisticated threat actor designated UAT-8616, using infrastructure overlapping with Operational Relay Box networks.
contents:
## Active Exploitation Campaign

Cisco Talos published on May 14 a detailed analysis of ongoing exploitation targeting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager systems. The primary vulnerability, CVE-2026-20182, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass authentication and gain administrative privileges. Talos attributed the exploitation to UAT-8616, assessed as a highly sophisticated cyber threat actor.

## Threat Actor Profile

UAT-8616 previously exploited CVE-2026-20127, a similar authentication bypass vulnerability that prompted a joint advisory from the NSA, CISA, and international partners in February 2026. The threat actor's post-compromise activities included adding SSH keys, modifying NETCONF configurations, and escalating to root privileges. Infrastructure used by UAT-8616 overlapped with Operational Relay Box (ORB) networks that Talos monitors.

## Broader Exploitation Landscape

Talos also identified ten distinct threat clusters exploiting a separate chain of previously disclosed vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-20133, CVE-2026-20128, CVE-2026-20122) following the public release of proof-of-concept code by ZeroZenX Labs in March. These clusters deployed various webshells including Godzilla, Behinder, and the ZeroZenX-associated XenShell, along with Sliver implants and XMRig cryptominers.

## Defensive Guidance

Organizations running Cisco SD-WAN technology should immediately apply patches for all disclosed CVEs, collect forensic artifacts and logs, hunt for evidence of compromise using indicators published by Talos, and implement the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Hardening Guide covering network perimeter controls, control plane security, and logging configurations.

link_preview.title: Ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities
link_preview.description: Cisco Talos is tracking the active exploitation of CVE-2026-20182, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage.

title: Xi-Trump Summit Day 2 Begins with Nvidia Chip Access and Iran Mediation Discussions in Beijing
summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump resumed talks in Beijing for the final day of their summit, with discussions covering Nvidia chip sales to Chinese firms, Iran war mediation, and trade expansion. Xi reportedly offered to help resolve the Strait of Hormuz crisis.
contents:
## Xi-Trump Summit Day 2 Developments

### Chip Access and Technology Talks
The second day of the Xi-Trump summit in Beijing opened with continuing discussions over semiconductor access and artificial intelligence cooperation. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, who joined the presidential delegation as a last-minute addition, attended bilateral meetings where chip export controls dominated conversations. The U.S. Commerce Department reportedly cleared approximately 10 Chinese firms to purchase Nvidia's H200 graphics processing units, including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com.

A senior U.S. official noted that China would need to make its own decisions regarding chip purchases, reflecting ongoing tensions between technology cooperation and national security concerns. The presence of major tech executives alongside Trump underscored the commercial dimension of the summit.

### Iran and Strait of Hormuz
Xi expressed willingness to assist in resolving the ongoing Iran conflict, telling Trump he would help, stating "if I could help in any way whatsoever, I would like to help." Both leaders agreed that Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon and that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open to support the free flow of energy. Xi also voiced China's opposition to the militarization of the strait and any effort to charge a toll for its use.

China expressed interest in purchasing more American oil to reduce its dependence on the strait, where transit volumes have dropped 97% since the conflict began. The White House characterized these discussions as productive, though analysts noted Beijing provided no firm commitments on pressuring Tehran.

### Taiwan Warning
Xi reiterated that the Taiwan question remained the "most important issue" in U.S.-China relations, warning that mishandling the situation could lead to "clashes and even conflicts" between the two nations. The warning came as the U.S. had delayed a $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan ahead of the visit.

### Trade and Boeing Deal
Trump announced that China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing 737 jets, marking Beijing's first order of U.S.-made commercial aircraft in nearly a decade. Both sides agreed to establish a "Board of Trade" mechanism to manage the economic relationship without reopening tariff negotiations.

title: KEI Analysis Warns Seoul Faces Hard Choices Between Alliance Obligations and Energy Security After Hormuz Attack
summary: The Korea Economic Institute published analysis examining South Korea's strategic dilemma following the HMM Namu attack, warning that the Iran war could trigger macroeconomic shock through rising oil prices, weakened won, and supply chain disruptions.
contents:
## KEI Strategic Assessment

### Alliance Pressure Mounting
The Korea Economic Institute of America published a detailed analysis examining how the May 4 attack on the HMM Namu has forced Seoul into difficult strategic calculations. President Trump pressed South Korea to join a U.S.-led maritime security mission in the Strait of Hormuz within hours of the explosion, demanding a choice the Lee Jae Myung administration had thus far avoided making.

### Energy Vulnerability Exposed
The analysis highlighted South Korea's acute dependence on the Strait of Hormuz for energy imports. Over 60 percent of the country's crude oil and 50 percent of its naphtha, a key petrochemical feedstock, transited through the strait in 2025. The semiconductor and display industries rely on Qatar for 64 percent of helium imports, amplifying concerns about supply chain shortages should the strait remain contested.

### Macroeconomic Risks
KEI warned of a looming cost-push inflation cycle in which rising oil and liquefied natural gas prices weaken the already pressured won and increase production, transportation, and energy costs across the economy. The longer instability persists in the strait, the higher shipping insurance premiums and freight costs will rise, potentially triggering a macroeconomic shock that slows growth and destabilizes the overall economy.

### Balancing Act
The analysis recommended Seoul pursue a calibrated approach, strengthening maritime security cooperation with the United States and partners while adopting flexible alliance strategies. Short-term options include non-combat roles such as maritime surveillance, intelligence sharing, and logistical support. The government has also partnered with Australia and North African countries to diversify crude oil supply away from the Middle East.

### China Factor
Enhanced cooperation with Washington on Hormuz security could prove sensitive for Seoul's relationship with Beijing, as such moves could expose South Korea to military risks, economic burdens, and worsening relations with China and Iran. The analysis noted this tension reflects Seoul's longstanding balancing act between its U.S. security alliance, Middle Eastern energy dependence, and Chinese economic ties.

title: Iran Crude Exports Halted for 28 Days as Kharg Island Loading Terminals Remain Empty
summary: Maritime intelligence firm TankerTrackers confirmed Iran has failed to export any crude oil by sea for 28 consecutive days under the U.S. naval blockade, while Kharg Island loading terminals showed no tanker activity since May 6 due to a suspected oil spill.
contents:
## Iran Export Shutdown Deepens

### Complete Crude Blockade
Maritime tracking firm TankerTrackers confirmed that Iran has not successfully exported any crude oil by sea over the past 28 days, defining an "export" as a tanker successfully crossing the U.S. Navy blockade line without returning with the cargo. The finding represented the longest continuous halt in Iranian crude exports since the conflict began on February 28.

### Kharg Island Disruption
Kharg Island, which handles 90 percent of Iran's oil exports, has not loaded any tankers since May 6, according to tracking data. Satellite imagery revealed a suspected oil spill spreading across approximately 45 square kilometers of water near the island between May 6 and May 8. Tehran denied any oil leak, but imagery from Copernicus Sentinel satellites showed gray-and-white slick patterns consistent with crude oil.

### Dark Fleet Adaptation
Despite the export halt, approximately 20 dark tankers remained staged in the Kharg waiting area with combined carrying capacity exceeding 25 million barrels. Windward Maritime Intelligence reported that dark vessel activity surged nearly 600 percent between April 19 and May 3, indicating sustained attempts to circumvent the blockade through concealed shipping operations.

### Refined Products Still Moving
While crude exports ceased, some refined petroleum products managed to leave Iranian waters because the U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control had not sanctioned the specific tankers involved in those trades. Multiple empty and loaded tankers remained clustered near the blockade perimeter and in waters near Pakistan.

### Market Impact
Brent crude futures remained elevated near $107 per barrel, with international benchmark prices having surged approximately 50 percent since the war began. The International Energy Agency projected global oil supply would fall by around 3.9 million barrels per day across 2026, with more than 1 billion barrels of Middle East supply already lost. The Pentagon put the total cost of the war at $29 billion, an increase of $4 billion from estimates provided late last month.

links.label: Iran International - Crude exports absent for 28 days

title: Windward Reports Kharg Island Export Disruption Deepens as IRGC Maritime Control Expands Across Hormuz
summary: Windward Maritime Intelligence reported that Kharg Island loading terminals stood empty for the first time since April 18, while IRGC fast craft deployments exceeded 342 vessels across five Hormuz sectors, indicating Iran increasingly controls portions of the strait as maritime holding zones.
contents:
## Windward Maritime Intelligence Assessment

### Kharg Island Empty
Satellite imagery collected between May 11 and May 13 showed no active tanker loading operations at Kharg Island for a third consecutive day. On May 11, all loading terminals appeared empty of tankers for the first time since April 18, despite approximately 19 staged dark tankers in the eastern waiting area carrying an estimated combined capacity exceeding 25 million barrels.

### No Confirmed Departures
No confirmed crude departures have occurred from Kharg since May 7. Windward assessed crude export volume during the week of May 4-10 at approximately 2.68 million barrels, well below normal levels. Separate satellite imagery identified visible structural disruption near the southwestern Kharg terminal area, with tugboat and barge activity suggesting ongoing repair operations associated with earlier structural damage.

### Dark Fleet Expansion
Dark tanker holding zones continued expanding near Larak, Qeshm, eastern Hormuz, and Chabahar. Persistent ship-to-ship transfer activity and prolonged dark anchorage behavior indicated growing covert staging operations inside Iranian territorial waters. Commercial vessel movement through Hormuz remained heavily restricted, with most large hulls stationary under dark or EMCON conditions.

### IRGC Presence Surges
IRGC-linked maritime surveillance and patrol activity intensified significantly. Approximately 342 high-speed craft operated across five monitored Hormuz sectors by May 13, substantially above the May 4-10 baseline. The northern corridor remained the dominant operating zone, with persistent concentrations near commercial choke points and Larak Island.

### Maritime Control Zones
Windward assessed that significant portions of the Strait now function as controlled maritime operating zones shaped by covert staging, surveillance, selective transit management, and constrained export activity. Iran appeared to manage maritime flows through a layered staging and control architecture rather than normal commercial transit patterns.

links.label: Windward Blog - Kharg Export Disruption Deepens

title: United Kingdom Awards Nearly One Billion Pound Contract for 72 RCH 155 Howitzers from German Defense Joint Venture
summary: The UK Ministry of Defence signed a nearly £1 billion contract with ARTEC, a KNDS-Rheinmetall joint venture, to procure 72 Remote Controlled Howitzers mounted on BOXER chassis, replacing AS90 systems donated to Ukraine and supporting at least 500 jobs across the UK.
contents:
## UK Artillery Modernization Contract

### Contract Details
The UK Ministry of Defence announced a nearly £1 billion contract to procure 72 Remote Controlled Howitzer 155 systems for the British Army, awarded through the Organisation for Joint Armament Cooperation to ARTEC, a joint venture between KNDS and Rheinmetall. The RCH 155 system fires eight rounds per minute at targets up to 70 kilometers away and mounts on a BOXER chassis capable of speeds up to 100 km/h.

### Industrial Benefits
The program supports at least 500 jobs across the United Kingdom. Weapon systems including the barrel, breech, recoil system and trunnions will undergo manufacture at Rheinmetall's large-calibre production facility in Telford, using British steel from Sheffield Forgemasters. The BOXER drive module will undergo production by KNDS UK in Stockport, sustaining armoured steel welding capabilities and supporting 100 skilled positions at each facility.

### Strategic Context
Defence Secretary John Healey described the investment as "defence delivering for the battlefield and for Britain's economy," emphasizing how securing next-generation artillery with Germany strengthens NATO against growing Russian aggression while creating highly skilled domestic jobs. German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius highlighted the contract as demonstrating serious NATO interoperability between London and Berlin.

### Trinity House Agreement
The procurement forms part of the UK-Germany Trinity House Agreement signed in October 2024, designed to strengthen bilateral defence cooperation and improve collective defence capabilities on NATO's eastern flank. Both ministers confirmed they continue implementing the arrangement step by step.

### Replacing Ukraine Donations
The RCH 155 replaces AS90 artillery systems transferred to Ukraine in 2023. The Archer artillery system serves as an interim capability until the new platform enters operational service. First deliveries arrive in 2028, with the British Army aiming to achieve an initial deployable capability before the end of the decade. The automated turret allows operation from the crew compartment by a crew of two soldiers, reducing vulnerability to enemy targeting.

title: Google Threat Intelligence Reveals North Korean and Chinese Hackers Exploit AI for Vulnerability Discovery at Scale
summary: Google's threat intelligence group reported that North Korean APT45 hackers deployed thousands of AI-driven recursive prompts to autonomously discover cybersecurity vulnerabilities, marking the first confirmed use of AI by state-sponsored actors for mass exploitation planning.
contents:
## State-Sponsored AI Cyber Threats

### Google Threat Intelligence Findings
Google's threat intelligence group released a report revealing that state-sponsored hackers from North Korea and China demonstrated "significant interest" in leveraging artificial intelligence to detect previously unknown cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The report confirmed that North Korean hacking group APT45 sent thousands of repetitive prompts that recursively analyze different security blind spots for possible exploitation.

### First Mass-Scale AI Exploitation
The incident marked the first time Google identified attackers using AI to find new vulnerabilities and exploit them on a mass scale. The recursive prompt pattern appeared designed to automate vulnerability analysis and attack-code testing, potentially accelerating the speed and sophistication of cyberattacks beyond what human-coded approaches could achieve.

### Kimsuky Malware Evolution
Moscow-based cybersecurity firm Kaspersky separately uncovered signs that the North Korean hacking group Kimsuky used a large language model in creating new malware. Researchers found emoji-filled comments and grammatical mistakes embedded in the code of the HelloDoor malware strain, patterns suggesting AI-assisted code generation. Kimsuky appeared to target Seoul's government public key infrastructure certification system, potentially enabling hackers to impersonate government officials and access internal state networks.

### Industry Response
The cybersecurity industry scrambled for countermeasures following Anthropic's release of Claude Mythos, an AI model specializing in detecting software security vulnerabilities. Microsoft announced its "Mdash" multi-model scanning harness achieved an industry-leading 88.45 percent score on the CyberGym benchmark of 1,507 real-world vulnerabilities, ahead of Claude Mythos Preview at 83.1 percent. Palo Alto Networks warned organizations have a narrow three-to-five-month window to outpace adversarial AI-driven exploits.

### National Security Implications
South Korean cybersecurity experts warned that AI-powered attacks could far eclipse dangers associated with human-coded hacking attempts. Professor Youm Heung-youl of Soonchunhyang University noted that North Korea could develop or access security-specialized AI systems, potentially deploying zero-day attacks that exploit previously undiscovered software vulnerabilities with no existing defense.
